summary: Large Jigglypuff/Purin Neutral-B Rollout decompilation pass. The PR replaced many ftPr_SpecialN.c placeholder stubs with C implementations for ground and air Neutral-B enter, anim, IASA, phys, coll, helper/callback paths, and added the type/header data needed to express those routines. decomp.dev reported +9540 matched bytes, 27 new 100% matches in main/melee/ft/chara/ftPurin/ftPr_SpecialN, plus several near-matches for remaining rollout release/turn physics and .sdata2. The author explicitly noted that some functions were not 100% but were close enough to be useful.
what_changed: Implemented a large set of previously stubbed ftPr_SpecialN.c routines for Purin/Jigglypuff Rollout, including ftPr_SpecialAirN_Enter, loop/full/release/turn/end/hit animation handlers, IASA handlers, ground/air physics handlers, collision handlers, and ftPr_SpecialN_8014222C.
Added helper inlines in ftPr_SpecialN.c such as setupPurinCallbacks, normalizeAndSetRollAngle, playRollSFX, scaleAnimStep, and hitCapsuleToggle to factor repeated rollout behavior.

Added rollout behavior around callback setup, angle normalization modulo 2π, TopN/YRotN part rotations, hit capsule enable/disable and damage updates, SFX triggering, JObj scale animation, slope-aware velocity handling, and ground/air transition behavior.
Expanded ftPurin_MotionVars.specialn in types.h from mostly unknown padding into typed fields x0, x4, x8, xC, x10, x14, x18, x1C, facing_dir, x24, x28, x2C, x30, and Vec3 x34.
Expanded ftPurinAttributes in types.h with many Neutral-B-related float/s32 fields from x4C through xD8 while preserving unknown padding where not yet typed.
Changed ftPr_Init data typing: ftPr_Init_803D05C8 and ftPr_Init_803D05D8 became f32[4] arrays rather than Vec4, and ftPr_Init_803D0610 became an ftCollisionBox rather than a Vec3 array.
Exposed the newly typed init data in ftPr_Init.h and added ftCommon/forward.h so ftCollisionBox could be declared.
Updated ftPr_SpecialN.h signature for ftPr_SpecialS_8013DD54 from one argument to HSD_GObj* plus bool arg1.
Added includes for common fighter fall/landing modules, cliff/collision/color animation, Purin init data, trigf, camera, efSync, and mpLib needed by the new implementations.
decomp.dev reported 27 new 100% matches in ftPr_SpecialN and 7 additional improved-but-unmatched items, but also reported 3 broken matches in ftKirby/ftKb_SpecialN.
smart_moves: Factored recurring callback assignment into setupPurinCallbacks, avoiding repeated assignments to death2_cb, take_dmg_cb, deal_dmg_cb, and x21F8 across motion-state changes.
Factored rollout angle wrapping and FtPart_YRotN rotation into normalizeAndSetRollAngle, matching the repeated modulo-2π pattern used by charge, release, turn, and hit paths.
Identified that the init scale constants are indexed as scalar arrays, not Vec4 structs, which made scaleAnimStep straightforward and improved .sdata2 matching.
Retyped ftPr_Init_803D0610 as ftCollisionBox so collision transition calls such as ft_800824A0(gobj, &ftPr_Init_803D0610) can use a semantic object instead of ambiguous Vec3 data.
Used targeted temporary padding and PAD_STACK/u8 scratch variables where needed to preserve stack layout for matching while still replacing stubs with readable C.
Kept motion flags as local static constants with @todo comments instead of over-naming uncertain bitfields, preserving matchability without claiming semantics that were not proven.
Modeled ground and air rollout variants with parallel structure, reusing shared helper logic for charge loops, full charge, release, turn, hit, and end paths.
decomp_lessons: When decompiling fighter specials, first type the motion-vars union and character attributes enough to express the routine; this PR shows that many ftPr_SpecialN matches depended on replacing anonymous padding with x-offset fields.
Data symbols used through array indexing should be typed as arrays even if their byte size resembles Vec4; incorrect aggregate types can block natural codegen and .sdata2 matching.
Collision box constants should use ftCollisionBox when passed to collision helpers; doing so improves both readability and call-site type correctness.
For fighter motion code, callbacks are often reset after Fighter_ChangeMotionState; a small helper can make repeated callback blocks easier to match and audit.
Angle normalization loops using M_PI * 2 and part rotation calls are a reusable pattern for rolling/spinning character states.
Partial decomp work can still be valuable when the match report shows many exact matches and the remaining nonmatches are isolated; this PR explicitly accepted several close-but-not-perfect routines.
Watch cross-unit match regressions after header/type changes: decomp.dev showed three broken ftKirby/ftKb_SpecialN matches despite the PR being focused on ftPurin.
naming_conventions: Purin/Jigglypuff special functions continue the project convention of address-suffixed names where semantics are not yet fully known, e.g. ftPr_SpecialS_8013D8E4, ftPr_SpecialS_8013DA24, ftPr_SpecialS_8013DC64, ftPr_SpecialS_8013DD54, and ftPr_SpecialN_8014222C.
New local helpers use lowerCamelCase semantic names such as setupPurinCallbacks, normalizeAndSetRollAngle, playRollSFX, scaleAnimStep, and hitCapsuleToggle.
Unidentified struct members remain offset-style xNN names in ftPurin_MotionVars and ftPurinAttributes rather than speculative semantic names.
Motion-state names follow existing ftPr_MS_SpecialN*/ftPr_MS_SpecialAirN* conventions, including left/right variants like ftPr_MS_SpecialNEndR and ftPr_MS_SpecialNEndL.
Extern data symbols keep address-style names such as ftPr_Init_803D05C8, ftPr_Init_803D05D8, and ftPr_Init_803D0610 until stronger symbolic names are known.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK and explicit u8 scratch arrays to influence stack layout in newly decompiled routines.
Kept exact float expressions and casts such as deg_to_rad multipliers, M_PI/M_PI_2 comparisons, and f32 casts around 0.2-derived formulas to fit code generation.
Used static u32 const motion-flag masks with raw bit shifts where named flag coverage was incomplete.
Introduced NEGABS(x) macro for absolute-value comparisons in places where direct fabsf may not have matched expected codegen.
Preserved separate ground and air branches even when logic is similar, likely to match original control flow around gr_vel, self_vel, and ground_or_air.
Used inline helpers for repeated code patterns while keeping them static inline so they can disappear at call sites and preserve expected assembly.
Retyped data declarations in ftPr_Init.c/h to match access patterns: f32[4] for scale tables and ftCollisionBox for collision bounds.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
Author note: "I prefer to only push 100% matches, but some of these felt close enough to be worth it."
decomp.dev bot reported +0.25% matched code and +9540 bytes, with 27 new 100% matches in ftPr_SpecialN.
decomp.dev bot also reported 3 broken matches in ftKirby/ftKb_SpecialN: ftKb_SpecialAirS_Enter, ftKb_SpecialHi1_Anim, and ftKb_EatJump1_Anim.

decomp.dev bot reported remaining improved-but-not-perfect ftPr_SpecialN items including ftPr_SpecialNRelease_Anim at 99.77%, ftPr_SpecialNTurn_Anim at 98.59%, ftPr_SpecialNTurn_Phys at 93.31%, ftPr_SpecialNRelease_Phys at 93.50%, ftPr_SpecialAirNChargeRelease_Phys at 97.92%, ftPr_SpecialAirNStartTurn_Phys at 97.14%, and .sdata2 at 98.81%.
follow_up_queries: Investigate why ftKirby/ftKb_SpecialN matches broke after this ftPurin-focused PR.

Finish or revisit the near-matching ftPr_SpecialNRelease_Anim, ftPr_SpecialNTurn_Anim, ftPr_SpecialNTurn_Phys, ftPr_SpecialNRelease_Phys, ftPr_SpecialAirNChargeRelease_Phys, and ftPr_SpecialAirNStartTurn_Phys routines.
Replace raw motion flag masks in ftPr_SpecialN.c with named flags once the flag meanings are confirmed.
Look for stronger semantic names for ftPurinAttributes x4C-xD8 and ftPurin_MotionVars.specialn x-fields as more Purin rollout behavior is understood.
Confirm whether ftPr_SpecialAirNChargeRelease_Coll remained a stub or only had its placeholder comment updated in this PR.
